title: Delete dead Daemon.Swarm.* (3,051 lines, 0 refs)

title: Retrospector -> Thompson Sampling feedback
description: When Retrospector accepts experiment, update PromptSelector variant alpha. Gate: after accepted experiment, variant alpha increases. Scope: M. Deps: none.

title: Fix health/model mismatch and investigate source degradation
description: Dogfooding found two remaining issues after the DecisionJournal stale-lock fix: 1) /health still reports glm-4.7 while the live runtime model command shows zhipu/glm-5.1, and 2) investigate degrades badly because alphaXiv MCP returns 401 Missing Authorization and Semantic Scholar is hitting 429 rate limits. Follow-up should fix model reporting and make investigate degrade faster/more cleanly under missing alphaXiv auth and source throttling.

title: Test investigate tool pure-logic paths (1,981 lines, 0 tests)
description: Create test/tools/investigate_test.exs — extract_keywords, dedup_papers, quality_score, classify_evidence_type, compute_verdict, format_report. 15+ tests, no LLM/network calls. Gate: mix test test/tools/investigate_test.exs. Scope: XL.

title: vaos-knowledge / vaos-ledger health monitoring
description: Extend health-check skill to assess vaos-knowledge (108 tests) and vaos-ledger (241 tests). Check: tests pass, no new warnings, dependency versions aligned with vaos-daemon. Gate: health report covers all 3 repos. Scope: M. Deps: #1 (beads init).

title: Bound remaining unbounded knowledge-graph scans outside Agent.Context
Test PACT/Negotiation (681 lines, 0 tests)
description: Create test/agent/orchestrator/negotiation_test.exs — quality gates, phase transitions, rollback, timeouts. Gate: 10+ tests pass. Scope: L.

title: Tune adaptation trials after clean pilot
description: After fixing trial terminal closure and DecisionJournal DateTime sanitization, rerun the clean DAEMON_EVAL_MODE adaptation pilot and judge the live outcomes. Current pilot result: baseline trials were started=15 completed=1 helpful=0 blocked=9 not_helpful=1; four fresh claims moved the loop to started=18 completed=4 helpful=0 blocked=9 not_helpful=4. Structural path now works: fresh trials were created, consumed with steering, and completed cleanly. But every completed trial in the pilot was classified not_helpful, leaving positive_signatures empty and low_verification signatures noisy. Diagnose whether the problem is steering content, quality scoring, or helpful/inconclusive/not_helpful thresholds before adding more autonomy.

comments.author: speed
comments.text: Root cause found and fixed: AdaptationTrials normalizes investigation payloads to string-keyed maps before scoring, but Retrospector.compute_quality/1 only read top-level atom keys and only counted source_type for narrow key/value combinations. That forced normalized trial_completed payloads to quality=0.0 and outcome=not_helpful. Fixed scorer mixed-key handling, added regressions, and verified with a real DecisionJournal smoke that now records trial_completed quality≈0.8 and outcome=helpful for the normalized payload shape.

title: Fix CostTracker SQLite schema + re-enable
description: Add Ecto schema for agent_budgets, re-enable in Extensions supervisor. Gate: CostTracker starts without crash. Scope: M. Deps: none.

title: Optimize GET /sessions global session listing
description: GET /api/v1/sessions still calls Memory.list_sessions(), which scans all session JSONL files through the memory GenServer. This was not on the hot path that caused the memory-block warning, but it remains a potential scalability bottleneck. Replace it with indexed metadata or a direct SQLite-backed listing path.

title: Inject DecisionLedger context into agent loop system prompt
description: Verify/wire context_block/0 into prompt assembly in loop.ex. Gate: system prompt includes tool reliability notes after 5+ observations. Scope: M. Deps: none.
close_reason: Already shipped: 0a5b222 closed-loop steering + 3f06896 LLM guidance for Tool Reliability Notes

title: Add thin adaptation heartbeat intent layer
description: Implement a small adaptation heartbeat process under the Adaptation supervisor that periodically inspects DecisionJournal meta-state, detects simple stagnation/conflict conditions, and records reflect/consolidate/pivot intents into the adaptation journal. Scope: journal-writing and observability only; no arbitration or direct policy rewriting.
close_reason: Shipped thin adaptation heartbeat under Adaptation supervisor with journal intent detection and tests

title: Investigate remains slow after parser/model fixes
description: Investigate still takes too long end-to-end after fixing adversarial parsing and model fallback issues. Remaining work: instrument post-paper phases, measure citation verification cost, and consider splitting adversarial vs verification models or capping sequential verification work.
notes: Investigate performance work remains separate; this session implemented adaptation instrumentation instead of latency optimization.

title: Fix consumed adaptation trials stuck awaiting outcome
description: After the heartbeat/trials intent-flow fix, a short live DAEMON_EVAL_MODE pilot showed the meta-layer engaging: a fresh pending trial was created on run 2 and successfully consumed onto the next claim on run 3. But the consumed trial stayed in status=awaiting_outcome instead of resolving to trial_completed or trial_blocked. The affected live example used claim 'regular exercise reduces cardiovascular disease risk'; consume_trial returned ok with applied_topic_key matching that claim, but AdaptationTrials.current_trial was still awaiting_outcome during run 4 and adaptation review counts only moved started=12->13 while completed/helpful/blocked stayed flat. Investigate why live Investigate/ActiveLearner/DecisionJournal event flow is not closing the consumed trial after observe_investigation or observe_failure.
close_reason: Investigate now closes consumed trials on direct conflict/error paths and emits completion artifacts for partial successes.

title: Dogfood adaptation trials and add promotion/suppression
description: Run live workloads against the new AdaptationTrials layer, inspect whether trials are helpful, expired, or noisy, and if trustworthy add bounded promotion/suppression so helpful trials can become temporary defaults and unhelpful ones cool down.

title: Rerun clean adaptation pilot after normalized trial scoring fix
description: Rerun the clean DAEMON_EVAL_MODE adaptation pilot after the normalized trial scoring fix. Confirm that fresh live trial_completed events no longer collapse to quality=0.0, inspect /status adaptation review for helpful/inconclusive/not_helpful movement, and only then decide whether any threshold or steering tuning is still needed.

comments.author: speed
comments.text: Pilot rerun was partially completed and exposed a methodological confound. Under DAEMON_EVAL_MODE=true, manual investigate runs completed, but they produced no new topic-scoped coordination events () even when AdaptationHeartbeat emitted fresh intents. Baseline review was started=19 completed=5 helpful=1 not_helpful=4 blocked=9; after two claims it moved only to started=20 completed=5 helpful=1 not_helpful=4 blocked=9. Root cause: eval mode disables ActiveLearner direct chaining, and AdaptationTrials.consume_trial/1 is only called from the direct chain path in active_learner.ex, so manual eval claims cannot consume pending trials.

comments.author: speed
comments.text: Final state after partial rerun: baseline review was started=19 completed=5 helpful=1 not_helpful=4 blocked=9; after two fresh manual claims it was started=20 completed=5 helpful=1 not_helpful=4 blocked=9. Claim 1 ('smoking causes lung cancer') finished in ~161600ms with Retrospector quality 0.0 and no topic-scoped coordination events. Claim 2 ('the earth is flat') finished in ~194372ms with Retrospector quality 0.0 and no topic-scoped coordination events. During claim 2, AdaptationHeartbeat emitted fresh reflect/pivot intents, so the system did create new coordination pressure, but the manual eval runs still could not consume a pending trial.

title: Upstream DecisionLedger + steering to public OSA
description: Port DecisionLedger, session failures, steering annotations from Daemon.* to OptimalSystemAgent.* namespace. Include tests. Gate: OSA repo compiles with DecisionLedger, tests pass. Scope: L. Deps: #6 (DecisionLedger prompt), #10 (escalation loop).

title: Port Swarm Intelligence to Orchestrator namespace
description: Port voting/convergence from dead Daemon.Swarm.Intelligence to Daemon.Agent.Orchestrator.SwarmIntelligence. Register as SwarmMode pattern :intelligence. Gate: 6+ tests, SwarmMode.launch(task, pattern: :intelligence) works. Scope: L. Deps: #4 (PACT/Negotiation tests), #5 (MockProvider).

title: Instrument adaptation journal across research and reliability loops
close_reason: Implemented durable adaptation journaling and derived meta-state, plus instrumentation across research and reliability loops.

title: Add ComfyUI ID-LoRA scene runner
Remove global session scan from GET /sessions/:id
Profile and reduce citation verification latency in investigate
description: A clean DAEMON_EVAL_MODE smoke on a fresh beetroot topic completed in 464307ms, with phase timings preflight=3357ms, search=5553ms, for_llm=51593ms, against_llm=26715ms, verify=348265ms, post=28818ms. Citation verification is now the dominant latency source by a wide margin. Next work should break verification down further, identify whether time is spent in per-citation provider calls, sequential verification, or retries, and then reduce or parallelize that path without degrading evidence quality.
notes: Reduced investigate citation verification latency by moving verification off the pinned reasoning model onto the provider utility tier, capping verification max_tokens, and emitting verification_stats in metadata and receipts. Focused tests passed: mix test test/tools/investigate_test.exs test/daemon/receipt/bundle_test.exs test/agent/active_learner_test.exs test/agent/scheduler/heartbeat_test.exs. Clean DAEMON_EVAL_MODE smoke on sodium bicarbonate topic completed in 133738ms with timings preflight=3170ms search=5322ms for_llm=23533ms against_llm=20786ms verify=54779ms post=26126ms; verification model=glm-4.5-flash, items=8, avg_llm=6847ms, slowest_llm=30050ms. Previous clean baseline was total=464307ms verify=348265ms, so verify dropped by ~84% and total runtime by ~71%.

title: Investigation pipeline integration tests
description: Full execute/1 with MockProvider, citation verification, two-store classification. @tag :integration, 8+ tests. Gate: mix test --include integration passes. Scope: L. Deps: #2 (investigate tests), #5 (MockProvider).

title: Fleet beads tool for autonomous issue work
description: beads_tool builtin wrapping bd CLI via shell_execute. Fleet.Registry.assign_issue/2 with agent capability matching. Gate: issue assignment test passes. Scope: L. Deps: #1 (beads init).

title: Add bounded adaptation steering trial layer
description: Next step after trustworthy heartbeat signals: add a thin AdaptationTrials service that turns trusted research meta intents into one-shot, auto-expiring steering trials. ActiveLearner should consume active trial steering once, the trial should record started/applied/completed-or-expired events into DecisionJournal, and status adaptation should expose the active trial if present. Keep scope bounded: research steering only, no arbitration or policy rewrite.

title: Test Learning engine (294 lines, 0 tests)
description: Create test/agent/learning_test.exs — observe, patterns, solutions, persistence. Follow DecisionLedger test_mode: true ETS isolation pattern. Gate: 8+ tests pass. Scope: M.

title: Replace Memory.list_sessions JSONL scan with SQLite-backed path
description: HTTP routes now avoid the global JSONL scan, but Daemon.Agent.Memory.list_sessions() and CLI /sessions still go through MiosaMemory.Store's do_list_sessions/1 file walk. Replace that broader API with a direct SQLite-backed listing path, with optional JSONL fallback only for legacy installs.

title: Heartbeat uses stale adaptation history as current state
description: Dogfood on fresh daemon shows /status adaptation immediately inherits old persisted authority_domain="reliability", bottleneck="low_verification", pivot_reason="smoke" and recent meta_reflect intent from old events. DecisionJournal.derive_meta_state/1 uses all persisted adaptation_entries without a freshness window, so AdaptationHeartbeat can emit intents from stale history.

title: Expose adaptation meta-state via HTTP/dashboard surfaces
description: Follow the shipped /status adaptation command with an operator-facing HTTP and/or dashboard surface for the adaptation journal and derived meta-state. Scope: read-only visibility first; no coordinator/arbitration yet.

title: Stabilize /machines command smoke tests without Fleet.Registry
description: test/commands_test.exs currently fails on /machines because Daemon.Fleet.Registry is not started in the test environment. Either start the registry in that test slice or make the command degrade gracefully when fleet services are absent.

title: Promote and suppress trusted adaptation trials
description: After adaptation trials proved trustworthy under success, blocked, and conflict paths, add bounded policy for helpful trial promotion and repeated not-helpful suppression. Promotion should be temporary and observable, and blocked trials should not count as evidence.

title: Diagnose inert adaptation heartbeat under clean workload
description: A clean post-latency-fix dogfood pilot produced fresh research adaptation events (quality_gate_skip and topic_selected) but no fresh meta intent or pending adaptation trial. AdaptationHeartbeat.tick_now left AdaptationTrials.current_trial nil before two real investigate runs, and adaptation review counts stayed flat at started=11 completed=1 helpful=0 blocked=7 expired=0. Investigate whether heartbeat trigger conditions are now too strict, looking specifically at why fresh research progress/failure signals with authority_domain=research and active_bottleneck=low_verification do not yield new meta_reflect/meta_pivot/meta_consolidate intents or bounded trials.
notes: Fixed the inert meta-layer in two places. First, AdaptationHeartbeat now emits meta_pivot_requested from the normal clean-workload pattern of one fresh research progress signal plus one fresh research failure under a live bottleneck (trigger=fresh_research_stagnation), instead of waiting for only repeated failures. Second, AdaptationTrials now accepts the real event-bus envelope shape where the adaptation signal lives under event.data, not just the raw stub payload used in tests. DecisionJournal now also counts quality_gate_skip as a failed adaptation so meta_state reflects fresh skips honestly. Verification: mix test test/intelligence/adaptation_trials_test.exs --no-start; mix test test/intelligence/adaptation_heartbeat_test.exs test/intelligence/decision_journal_adaptation_test.exs --no-start. Live smoke: recording a coordination meta_pivot_requested produced AdaptationTrials.current_trial = %{status: :pending, trigger_event: "meta_pivot_requested", bottleneck: "low_verification"} and recent journal types included trial_started.

title: Expose adaptation meta-state and journal via API/dashboard
description: Add read access to the new adaptation journal and derived meta-state through an internal API and/or dashboard surface so operators can inspect research vs reliability authority without reading persisted JSON directly.
close_reason: Shipped thin /status adaptation read surface; HTTP/dashboard follow-up moved to vas-swarm-let

title: Bound Agent.Context knowledge retrieval to avoid 5s timeouts
description: Replace unbounded knowledge-block scans with bounded candidate retrieval and prevent embedding health-check retries from consuming the context budget.
close_reason: Shipped: bounded Agent.Context knowledge retrieval + no-retry embedding probe; live Mini dogfood no longer times out knowledge_block.

title: Dogfood promoted adaptation defaults under live workload
description: Run VAOS with the new promotion/suppression layer enabled, inspect /status adaptation over real workloads, and tune thresholds or cooldown TTLs if promoted steering proves noisy or suppressions are too sticky. This follows the bounded adaptation trial work in vas-swarm-nkw.
notes: Clean DAEMON_EVAL_MODE pilot rerun after 43e956f moved the loop from structural debugging into judgment. Baseline trial review before the run was started=15 completed=1 helpful=0 blocked=9 not_helpful=1. Four fresh claims produced three fresh consumed trials with steering applied and clean terminal closure; final review ended at started=18 completed=4 helpful=0 blocked=9 not_helpful=4. So the self-evolution loop now creates, consumes, and completes trials under live workload, but every completed trial in this pilot was judged not_helpful. The pilot also exposed a DecisionJournal sanitize_context crash when suppression metadata included a DateTime expires_at; that was fixed immediately in the same session. Follow-up is now focused on steering/quality judgment tuning rather than terminal-path integrity.
close_reason: Clean pilot rerun completed; follow-up vas-swarm-61a now tracks diagnosing why all completed trials are judged not_helpful and tuning adaptation thresholds/steering accordingly.

title: Skill retirement/pruning for SkillEvolution
description: prune_failing_skills/0: failure_rate > 70% AND used >= 10 → archive to ~/.daemon/skills/retired/. Emit :skill_retired on Bus, 30-min timer. Gate: test proves failing skill archived + unregistered. Scope: M. Deps: #2 (investigate tests).

title: Make adaptation pilot measurable when eval mode disables chaining
description: The post-scoring-fix pilot showed that DAEMON_EVAL_MODE=true suppresses ActiveLearner direct chaining, but AdaptationTrials.consume_trial/1 is only invoked from the chain path. Manual investigate claims can therefore generate fresh heartbeat intents and even increment trial_started, but cannot consume a pending trial or produce topic-scoped trial_completed/trial_blocked outcomes. Add a pilot harness or code path that lets manual evaluation runs apply pending trial steering without re-enabling the full chain loop, then rerun the adaptation pilot.

comments.author: speed
comments.text: Evidence from the rerun: eval mode keeps heartbeat/meta-intent generation alive, but manual investigate claims do not traverse the AdaptationTrials.consume_trial path because that call lives in ActiveLearner.maybe_chain_investigation. This makes DAEMON_EVAL_MODE=true unsuitable for measuring live trial_completed outcomes unless we add a separate consumption path or a dedicated pilot harness.

title: Shared test helpers for GenServer isolation
description: Create test/support/ets_isolation.ex, temp_dir.ex, bus_capture.ex. Gate: DecisionLedger tests refactored to use shared helpers. Scope: S. Deps: #2, #3, #4 (test issues).

Produces structured health report, optionally creates beads issues for regressions. Gate: running skill produces report with test count, compile status, coverage gaps. Scope: M. Deps: #1 (beads init), #2 (investigate tests).
